def: Becoming a woman; feminine; as, womanly behavior. Arbuthnot.A blushing, womanly discovering grace. Donne.
A call or summons; especially, a judicial call, demand, ororder; as, the invocation of papers or evidence into court.
One of a celebrated breed of American trotting horses; -- socalled from the name of the stud from which the breed originated inVermont.
The act of darkening, or state of being dark; darkness;obscurity. Johnson.
The quality or condition of being a duke; also, the personalityof a duke. Massinger.
Pertaining to the Pomacentridæ, a family of bright-coloredtropical fishes having spiny opercula; -- often called coral fishes.
A compound of chloral and formic amide used to produce sleep.
An invariable quantity; specifically, a function of thecoefficients of one or more forms, which remains unaltered, whenthese undergo suitable linear transformations. J. J. Sylvester.
A nerve or tendon, supposed to brace and sustain the heart.Shak.Sobbing, as if a hearstring broke. Moore.
See Bone black, under Bone, n.
